message:
feat(ftui-web): add StepProgram WASM step-based runner (bd-lff4p.3.4)

Implements a host-driven, non-blocking program runner for wasm32 that
drives ftui Models through init/event/update/view/present cycles without
threads or blocking. The runner integrates with WebBackend for
deterministic clock, event source, and buffer capture.

Also fixes compute_diff compilation error in ftui-runtime's WasmRunner
(BufferDiff::compute is the correct API).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1362,8 +1362,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","title":"Implement: new crate ftui-web (WASM API surface + glue)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/ftui-web` exposing a minimal embedding API for running ftui models in WASM.

API Sketch
- `WasmApp::new(opts)`
- `resize(cols, rows)`
- `input(event_bytes or semantic event)`
- `tick(dt)`
- `render() -> Patch or Frame`

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p ftui-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.10","title":"Docs: embedding ftui-web + frankenterm-web into frankentui_website","description":"Goal
- Provide exact integration instructions for the Next.js site (bun) to consume the wasm artifacts.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear build steps, file locations, and runtime initialization.
- No reliance on xterm.js.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletSparrow","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:57.912485837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:09.317042295Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:09.317023310Z","close_reason":"Added Next.js+bun embedding docs for ftui-web and frankenterm-web","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:57.912485837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:57.912485837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:57.912485837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","title":"Refactor: runtime backend abstraction (no direct Crossterm usage)","description":"Goal
- Introduce a backend abstraction so ftui-runtime can run on:
  - native tty backend (custom)
  - wasm backend (JS-driven)

Acceptance Criteria
- `Program` no longer owns a concrete `TerminalSession` tied to Crossterm.
- Backends provide: size, input events (or bytes), present output (or patches), and mode toggles.
- Old code paths removed after migration (no lingering shims).

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.3.3","title":"Build: make ftui core crates wasm32-friendly (cfg gates, no unix-only deps)","description":"Goal
- Ensure the ftui crates required for demos compile for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`.

Scope
- Remove / cfg-gate: signal handling, raw mode, threads, std::process usage.
- Ensure deterministic time sources can be injected.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check --workspace --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds for the relevant crate set.

Key changes: (1) ftui-extras made ftui-runtime optional (4ca80e69), (2) ftui umbrella added runtime/extras feature gates (4ca80e69), (3) ftui-runtime crossterm-compat feature gate (4cf3df1b/e01f110f). Acceptance criteria verified: cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown passes for all crates.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:34:53Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","title":"Implement: WASM step-based Program runner (no blocking poll, no threads)","description":"Goal
- Implement a deterministic, JS-driven runtime loop for WASM.

Requirements
- Inputs are delivered from JS (keyboard/mouse/resize/ticks).
- Rendering produces either ftui-render Buffer diffs or FrankenTerm patches.
- No `std::thread` assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can drive an ftui Model with: init -> input -> view -> present cycles.
- Deterministic record/replay possible.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","title":"Integrate: ftui-render Buffer -> FrankenTerm patch feed","description":"Goal
- Connect ftui-render output to FrankenTerm so the browser renderer can present ftui frames efficiently.

Options
- Convert ftui-render Buffer diffs into FrankenTerm patch representation.
- Or define a shared patch protocol used by both.

Acceptance Criteria
- No full-frame copies on steady-state updates.
- End-to-end demo updates are visibly smooth.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","title":"Demo: run ftui-demo-showcase in browser via ftui-web + FrankenTerm","description":"Goal
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` fully client-side in the browser via `ftui-web` + `frankenterm-web` (no xterm.js).

Scope
- Boot, render, navigate, resize, and respond to keyboard + mouse with low latency.
- Any OS-dependent features must either:
  - have explicit web implementations, OR
  - be explicitly disabled with user-visible UX (no silent partial failure).
- Perf + stability: no leaks, no jank, and predictable frame-time under typical demo workloads.

Testing + Logging (Non-negotiable)
- Create a browser "demo sweep" analogous to `tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh`:
  - emits detailed JSONL (screen_id, seed, size, DPR, timings, frame/patch hashes)
  - failures emit minimal repro traces that can be replayed
- Determinism: recorded sessions replay to identical hashes on the same build.

Acceptance Criteria
- A single web page can run the demo for 10+ minutes without runaway memory or degraded responsiveness.
- JSONL logs validate against the shared schema and are rich enough to debug mismatches.
- Seed/time-freeze determinism is preserved end-to-end.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:19.049678331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:06.377151045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:06.710181461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.7","title":"Determinism: record/replay web sessions (input + resize + ticks)","description":"Goal
- Ensure we can deterministically reproduce bugs and perf issues in the browser.

Requirements
- Record: input events, resize events, tick deltas, and any nondeterminism sources.
- Replay: drive ftui-web runner to exact same rendered output checksums.

Acceptance Criteria
- A recorded session replays to identical frame checksums on same build.
